explanation:
$\because \frac{x^2+y^2}{x^2-y^2}+\frac{x^2-y^2}{x^2+y^2}=k$

$$\begin{array}{ll} \Rightarrow & \frac{\left(x^2+y^2\right)^2+\left(x^2-y^2\right)^2}{\left(x^2-y^2\right)\left(x^2+y^2\right)}=k \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{2\left(x^4+y^4\right)}{\left(x^4-y^4\right)}=k \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{x^4+y^4}{x^4-y^4}=\frac{k}{2} \quad\text{.... (i)}\\ \text { and } & \frac{x^4-y^4}{x^4+y^4}=\frac{2}{k}\quad\text{.... (ii)} \end{array}$$

$$\begin{aligned} &\text { Adding Eqs. (i) and (ii), }\\ &\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{x^4+y^4}{x^4-y^4}+\frac{x^4-y^4}{x^4+y^4}=\frac{k}{2}+\frac{2}{k} \\ & \Rightarrow \frac{\left(x^4+y^4\right)^2+\left(x^4-y^4\right)^2}{\left(x^4+y^4\right)\left(x^4-y^4\right)}=\frac{k^2+4}{2 k} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{2\left(x^8+y^8\right)}{\left(x^8-y^8\right)}=\frac{k^2+4}{2 k} \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

$\Rightarrow \quad \frac{x^8+y^8}{x^8-y^8}=\frac{k^2+4}{4 k}$
